author: writer-1
rationale: Input pin only (P2 P-9): resolves the 'PINNED-AT-INPUTS:gpt-5.5-catalog-slug-verified-and-pinned-at-staging-via-npx-codex-0.144.1...' placeholder. MEASURED at staging 2026-07-10: the staged npx codex 0.144.1 CLI's 'debug models' listing (byte-exact command spelling preserved in the manifest value and in judges-invocation.md section 4.2) shows the catalog slug exactly 'gpt-5.5' (supported reasoning efforts low/medium/high/xhigh, default medium; visibility list, supported_in_api true); the same staged CLI's '--version' printed exactly 'codex-cli 0.144.1'; the GLOBAL codex install printed 'codex-cli 0.142.5' both before and after the npx invocations — the global install was never touched, honoring the frozen runner constraint while f2b judge runs are in flight. Reasoning effort 'low' is STIPULATED (ASM-0241): the fixed minimal-reasoning contract of the judge-1p invocation form that the frozen record cites for judge-p1, applied uniformly to both codex judges (the frozen record pins the slug but not the effort; low is the most deterministic level the interface exposes and matches the f2b-line judge-2 diagnostic run). The runner preflight (judges-invocation.md section 8) gates the exec path fail-closed before any real item. One-shot pin discipline applies. RE-ISSUE + VALUE RECIPE (correction seq 3): this amendment was re-issued 2026-07-10 with only the patch value, the date, and this appended paragraph changed (plus two command spellings in this rationale restated in the scope-free form amendment seq 1 already uses; the byte-exact spellings live in the manifest value) — the verdict-spine overlay guard (tools/registry/verdict-gen.py PIN_VALUE_RE) lawfully requires every post-freeze ops pin value to be a bare 40- or 64-char lowercase hex digest, and the prose-valued original of this amendment (file sha256 ae3f58f23b9e7286265038b40aed4c877d15480c5b8bb71bb16983711865d2fa, preserved in git history at the pre-reissue commit) never survived any overlay, so nothing ever consumed it. The value is now, per the f2 amendment-6 / truthstyle amendment-1 staged-manifest convention extended to model_revisions (ASM-0440): sha256 over the canonical JSON (sort_keys, compact separators, UTF-8) of the {judge-key: model-revision-prose} dict committed verbatim as poc/truthstyle-2x2/model-revisions-manifest.json (canonical bytes + one trailing newline; that file's own sha256 = 80bcd310362b871f4ae39e0c0faea851f1a2d0fa7fe03a0f5ee433a5b8373fe9). The manifest's judge-p2-gpt55 value is BYTE-IDENTICAL to this amendment's superseded prose value (including every byte-exact command spelling), so the pin CONTENT is unchanged — merely relocated to a committed file and committed to by digest. Amendments seq 2 and seq 3 intentionally carry the IDENTICAL digest: both commit to the same manifest (precedent: f2 amendment seq 1 fills two corpus pins with one digest). One-shot pin discipline extends to the manifest bytes: any change before the final-phase run invalidates this value and requires a pre-run correction record, not a new ops amendment.
